title: Enclosure Line
source_text:
11. Enclosure Line

a. General. List enclosures in the enclosure line in the order they appear in the text. Identify an enclosure using the same format as you would when identifying a reference. See paragraph 10 above. When identifying a document, cite its subject or title exactly. Never list an item in both the enclosure line and reference line of the same letter.

b. Format. Type "Encl:" at the left margin on the second line below the last line of the previous heading. Three spaces follow the colon. Use a number in parentheses before the description of every enclosure, even if you have only one. One space follows the closing parenthesis. If the entry is longer than one line, start the second line under the first word after the heading.

c. Normal Distribution and When to Vary It. Normally, send one copy of the basic letter plus any enclosures to all addressees. Do not use the term "w/encl" to indicate that all addressees are being sent the enclosures. Avoid sending an enclosure if an addressee has it already or if its bulk or other factors make furnishing it impractical.

d. Adding Copies of Enclosures for all Addressees. When sending more than one copy of an enclosure to all addressees ("To," "Via," and "Copy to"), note the quantity in parentheses after the enclosure's description.

e. Variations Affecting Only Copy To Addressees. In certain circumstances, it may be necessary to vary the normal distribution of enclosures to "Copy to" addressees. It is up to the originator of the correspondence to make this determination. There are two basic methods of varying distribution; all "Copy to" addressees are affected or only one or a few of the "Copy to" addressees are affected.

(1) In these examples, all "Copy to" addressees are affected in the same way, so notes appear beside the "Copy to" heading.

(2) In this example, only some of the "Copy to" addressees are affected, so notes appear beside the affected addressees.

f. Variations Affecting Only Via Addressees. When varying the normal distribution of enclosures to "Via" addressees, show the variation beside the affected addressee. One possible variation appears below. Others may be adapted from the examples in paragraph 11e above.

g. Sending Enclosures Separately. When size, weight, or other factors prevent sending an enclosure with a letter, send it separately and type the term "sep cover" in parentheses after the enclosure's description.
plain_language_rule: A single enclosure IS numbered. Use a number in parentheses before every enclosure, even if only one — e.g., Encl:   (1) Report. List enclosures in the order they appear in the text. Identify an enclosure using the same format as a reference. Cite document subject or title exactly. Never list the same item as both reference and enclosure. Type "Encl:" at the left margin on the second line below the last line of the previous heading. Three spaces follow the colon. One space follows the closing parenthesis. Continuation lines start under the first word after the heading. Normal distribution sends one copy of the basic letter plus enclosures to all addressees. Do not use "w/encl" when all addressees receive enclosures. Use quantity notation when sending more than one copy of an enclosure to all addressees. Use Copy to/Via annotations when enclosure distribution varies. Use "(sep cover)" when sending an enclosure separately.
renderer: The enclosure line must be rendered at the left margin, on the second line below the last line of the previous heading. Enclosure items are numbered sequentially starting from (1), even when only one enclosure exists. Each number appears in parentheses, followed by one space, then the enclosure description. Continuation lines begin under the first word after the "Encl:" heading (i.e., under the first enclosure description word). Three spaces follow the colon after "Encl:". Distribution variations (w/o encls, w/encl, sep cover) are annotated per the manual examples at paragraph 7-2.11e, 7-2.11f, and 7-2.11g.

examples.notes: Even a single enclosure receives the number (1).

examples.expected_output:
Encl:   (1) List of Reserve Officers Selected for Promotion to Colonel
           (2) CMC ltr 5216 Ser 00/451 of 5 Sep 09
examples.notes: Continuation line (2) starts under the first word after "Encl:" heading.
Use "(sep cover)" when the enclosure is not physically included with the letter.

notes: This rule consolidates the full enclosure line specification from section 7-2.11, subsections a through g. A single enclosure always receives the number (1). Distribution annotations (w/o encls, w/encl, sep cover) must follow the manual's Copy to and Via patterns exactly. Renderer-specific layout details (exact spacing, continuation line alignment) are included for automated processing.
